revision.text:
{{Italic title|reason=[[:Category:Japanese words and phrases]]}}
''Sukune'' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>.
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''Mahito'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the [[Kami|''kami'']] (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>.
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|url-status=live|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>.
== References == <references /> [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese historical terms]] [[Category:Titles]]

It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=[[:Category:Japanese words and phrases]]}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=[[:Category:Japanese words and phrases]]}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==
* ''[[Ason]]'' * ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' * ''[[Muraji]]''

In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=[[:category:kk]][[category:Japanese words and phrases]]}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==
* ''[[Ason]]'' * ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' * ''[[Muraji]]''

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=[[:category:kk]][[category:Japanese words and phrases]]}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=[[:Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese words and phrases]]}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=[[:Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese words and phrases]]}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==

== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=[[:Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese words and phrases]]}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=[[:Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese words and phrases]]}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==
* ''[[Ason]]'' * ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' * ''[[Muraji]]''
== References == <references /> [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese historical terms]] [[Category:Titles]] [[Category:Japanese nobility]] [[Category:Kabane]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukune]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]][[Category:11 6]] [[en:Sukune]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Sukune]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukune]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]][[Category:11 6]] [[en:Sukune]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukune]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]][[Category:11 6]] [[en:Sukune]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Sukune]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukune]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]][[Category:11 6]] [[en:Sukune]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukune]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]][[Category:11 6]] [[en:Sukune]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Sukune]][[Category:11]] [[en:Sukune]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[en:Sukune]][[Category:kk]][[en:Sukune]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[en:Sukune]][[Category:11]] [[en:Sukune]][[en:Sukune]][[en:Sukune]][[en:Sukune]][[en:Sukune]][[en:Sukune]][[en:Sukune]]

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=[[:Category:kk]]}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==
* ''[[Ason]]'' * ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' * ''[[Muraji]]''
== References == <references />
]] ]]
{{Japan-hist-stub}}

[[sn:Sukune]] [[en:Sukune]] [[sn:ukune]] [[sn: failed]]

revision.comment: Bot: classify by valid enwiki interwikis
revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[3rd century|3rd]] to [[5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==
* ''[[Ason]]'' * ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' * ''[[Muraji]]''
== References == <references />
]] ]]
{{Japan-hist-stub}}

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[Nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[Japan]]. In the [[:en:3rd century|3rd]] to [[:en:5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[:en:8th century|8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==
* ''[[Ason]]'' * ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' * ''[[Muraji]]''
== References == <references />
]] ]]
{{Japan-hist-stub}}

[[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:宿禰]] [[ko:스쿠네]] [[uk:Сукуне]] [[zh:宿禰]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11455653]] Wikidata name: Sukune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[:en:Japanese nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. In the [[:en:3rd century|3rd]] to [[:en:5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[:en:Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[:en:8th century|8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[:en:Inariyama Sword|Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==
* ''[[Ason]]'' * ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' * ''[[Muraji]]''
== References == <references /> ]]

<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:宿禰]] [[ko:스쿠네]] [[uk:Сукуне]] [[zh:宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q11455653}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sukune]][[ff:Sukune]][[ja:宿禰]][[ko:스쿠네]][[uk:Сукуне]][[zh:宿禰]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11455653]] Wikidata name: Sukune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[:en:Japanese nobility|noble]] titles of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. In the [[:en:3rd century|3rd]] to [[:en:5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[:en:Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[:en:8th century|8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[:en:Inariyama Sword|Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==
* ''[[Ason]]'' * ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' * ''[[Muraji]]''
== References == <references /> ]]

In the [[:en:8th century|8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.

The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[:en:Inariyama Sword|Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

In the [[:en:3rd century|3rd]] to [[:en:5th century|5th centuries]], it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the [[:en:Yamato period|Yamato]] court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the [[:en:8th century|8th century]], it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo]] clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[:en:Inariyama Sword|Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==
* ''[[Ason]]'' * ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' * ''[[Muraji]]''
== References == <references /> ]]

The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient [[:en:Inariyama Sword|Inariyama Sword]] with the inscription "多加利足尼". It is said that during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

It is said that during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sukune]][[ff:Sukune]][[ja:宿禰]][[ko:스쿠네]][[uk:Сукуне]][[zh:宿禰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sukune]][[ff:Sukune]][[ja:宿禰]][[ko:스쿠네]][[uk:Сукуне]][[zh:宿禰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sukune]][[ff:Sukune]][[ja:宿禰]][[ko:스쿠네]][[uk:Сукуне]][[zh:宿禰]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11455653]] Wikidata name: Sukune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[:en:Japanese nobility|noble]] titles of ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. In the {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd}} to {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th centuries}}, it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato}} court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the {{ill|8th century|en|8th century|WD=Q8086|lt=8th century}}, it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo}} clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji}}'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient {{ill|Inariyama Sword|en|Inariyama Sword|WD=Q856395|lt=Inariyama Sword}} with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sukune]][[ff:Sukune]][[ja:宿禰]][[ko:스쿠네]][[uk:Сукуне]][[zh:宿禰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sukune]][[ff:Sukune]][[ja:宿禰]][[ko:스쿠네]][[uk:Сукуне]][[zh:宿禰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sukune]][[ff:Sukune]][[ja:宿禰]][[ko:스쿠네]][[uk:Сукуне]][[zh:宿禰]]
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary noble title}} {{Italic title|reason=}} '''''Sukune''''' (宿禰) is one of the hereditary [[:en:Japanese nobility|noble]] titles of ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. In the {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd|1=3rd century}} to {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th centuries|1=5th century}}, it was used as a title to represent military and administrative officers of the {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato period}} court.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Kanai|first=Seiichi|title=Sukunahi Kona no Meigi to Honshitsu (スクナヒ コナの名義と本質)|publisher=Tokyo Woman's Christian University|year=1971}}</ref>
In the {{ill|8th century|en|8th century|WD=Q8086|lt=8th century|1=8th century}}, it became one of the eight ''[[kabane]]''. It is the third highest after ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' and ''[[Ason]]''. It was given to the descendants of the ''[[kami]]'' (神別, ''shinbetsu''), who held the ''[[Muraji]]'' title, such as [[Owari clan|Owari]] and {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo|1=Ōtomo clan}} clans.
== Usage == In the past, the ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji|1=Kanji}}'' writing form used for Sukune was "足尼" or "足禰". The oldest known usage of Sukune is on the ancient {{ill|Inariyama Sword|en|Inariyama Sword|WD=Q856395|lt=Inariyama Sword|1=Inariyama Sword}} with the inscription "多加利足尼". In ''Kokuzō Hongi'', a book about the genealogy of [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] families, there are over 20 Yamato court officials with the Sukune title.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sendai Kuji Hongi (Kujiki), Volume 10 Kokuzo Hongi}}</ref>
== Sukune and Ōne titles == In ''[[Kujiki]],'' there are several cases in which the title of Sukune is given alongside the title of Ōne. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, Takeigokoro (建胆心命) was given the title of Ōne, and Tabe (多辨命) was given the title of Sukune.<ref>{{Cite web|title=『先代旧事本紀』の現代語訳 Online text of Kujiki in modern Japanese.|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~xx8f-ishr/sendaikuji_mokuji.htm|access-date=2021-03-13|website=www.asahi-net.or.jp}}</ref>
== See also ==
* ''[[Ason]]'' * ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'' * ''[[Muraji]]''
== References == <references /> ]]
{{Japan-hist-stub}}
